|affiliation=[[TransGal circuit]]<ref name="TransGal">Per [[Serji-X Arrogantus]]'s entry in the [[Databank (original)|Databank]], [https://web.archive.org/web/http://www.starwars.com/databank/character/serjixarrogantus/index.html archived here], Arrogantus raced in the [[Swo-O'Rim racing circuit]]. The HoloNet News article stated that the race on Corulag was a race in which both TransGal and Swo-O'Rim teams competed. It also established that Ominaz and Arrogantus were in different circuits; therefore, Team Millennium Holos had to compete in TransGal.</ref>
}}
{{Quote|The Ominaz win, combined with the fourth place showing of Dretti Grond, also restores Team Millennium Holos to top spot in the team rankings.|HoloNet News article|<ref name="Article">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|54|sports/1352_1.html|Ominaz Keeps Top Ranking}}</ref>}}
'''Team Millennium Holos''' was a professional [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop racing]] team that competed on the [[TransGal circuit]]. Although it once reigned as the top-ranked team in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], it had lost that status by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the team competed in a [[Tasjon swoop race|race]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]], and strong performances from its members [[Ignar Ominaz]] and [[Dretti Grond]] propelled Team Millennium Holos back to the top ranking.

==History==
At one point, Team Millennium Holos was the top-ranked professional [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop racing]] team in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], competing on the [[TransGal circuit]], but it had fallen from first place by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], its members included racers named [[Ignar Ominaz]] and [[Dretti Grond]], both of whom competed in a [[Tasjon swoop race|race]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]]. Ominaz's win there, along with Grond's fourth place finish, restored Team Millennium Holos' status as the top team. After the race on Corulag, the Holos planned to take part in the next race in the circuit, the [[Stassia Classic]].<ref name="Article" />

==Organization==
Team Millennium Holos consisted of associated [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], including Ignar Ominaz and Dretti Grond.<ref name="Article" /> Its members raced cooperatively on a galactic swoop racing circuit called the TransGal circuit.<ref name="TransGal" />

==Behind the scenes==
Team Millennium Holos was featured in an issue of the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] [[HoloNet News (original website)|feed]] ([[2002]]), [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 54|Volume 531, Issue 54]], which was published [[in-universe]] on the second day of the fifth [[Standard month/Legends|month]] in 22 BBY. The article that referenced the team, entitled "Ominaz Keeps Top Ranking," was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]].<ref name="Article" /> [[Millennium Holos]] was a [[Hologram/Legends|holo]] production company [[Canon|previously established]] in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' galaxy with the ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'', written in [[1993]] by [[Michael Allen Horne]], but a definitive connection between that company and the team was not established in HoloNet News.<ref name="han">''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]''</ref>
